data.title: RPC expands Permian operations with $245M acquisition of Pintail Completions
data.description:
RPC Inc. (NYSE:RES) said Wednesday it agreed to acquire Permian Basin provider of oilfield wireline services Pintail Completions for $245M in cash and stock.

Pintail operates more than 30 active fleets, and its conventional and electric wireline units are among the newest in the industry, and its FY 2024 revenues totaled $409M, according to RPC (NYSE:RES).

RPC (RES) said it expects the acquisition will be accretive 2025 earnings per share and operating and free cash flow.

"Pintail Completions is recognized as an industry leader in wireline perforation services, complementing our existing completion service lines and making wireline a meaningful component of RPC's portfolio," President and CEO Ben Palmer said.

data.title: Allegion acquires Trimco Hardware
data.description:
• Allegion (NYSE:ALLE) said it has acquired privately held Trimco Hardware, including its brands and other assets.
  • The terms of the deal were not disclosed. 
  • Trimco will report into the Allegion Americas segment, which is led by Allegion senior vice president Dave Ilardi.
  • The firm added Jason Bennett, owner of Trimco, will join Allegion as general manager of Trimco.

data.title: BlackBerry beats top-line and bottom-line estimates; initiates Q1 and FY26 outlook
data.description:
• BlackBerry press release (NYSE:BB): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.03 beats by $0.03.
  • Revenue of $141.7M (-18.1% Y/Y) beats by $8.89M.

  • Operating cash flow beat expectations and improved by $57 million year-over-year to $42.0 million.

    • Total company adjusted EBITDA exceeded previously-provided guidance at $21.1 million.

  • Total cash and investments increased by $144 million sequentially to $410 million, in part due to the improved operating cash flow, and in part due to approximately $80 million received from first tranche of cash from the sale of Cylance to Arctic Wolf.

  • Financial Outlook

data.title: Nayax acquires Inepro Pay
data.description:
• Nayax (NASDAQ:NYAX) said it has acquired Inepro Pay, a unit of Inepro and a Nayax distributor in the Benelux region.
  • The acquisition will help the firm expand its reach in the Benelux region.
  • "Inepro Pay has cultivated an unparalleled understanding of our products and customers as our distributor since 2015 in the Benelux region, and we’re confident that integrating their team and back-end operations will produce remarkable outcomes for businesses across Europe," said CEO Yair Nechmad.

data.title: Cognyte Software beats top-line and bottom-line estimates; initiates FY26 outlook
data.description:
• Cognyte Software press release (NASDAQ:CGNT): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.03 beats by $0.02.
  • Revenue of $94.51M (+12.9% Y/Y) beats by $1.36M.
  • Q4 FYE25 Adjusted EBITDA increased by approximately 114% to $9.3 million, compared to $4.3 million in the same period last year, demonstrating the leverage we have in our financial model.
  • Q4 FYE25 Billings(2) were $95.0 million, consistent with last year.
  • Total Backlog(3) at the end of Q4 FYE25 was $415.5 million and short-term Backlog was $227.9 million.
  • Total RPO(4) $545.8 million at the end of Q4 FYE25.
  • Short-term RPO(4) at the end of Q4 FYE25 increased to $335.3 million, providing solid visibility into FYE26 revenue.

  • FYE26 Outlook

    Our outlook for the year ending January 31, 2026 (“FYE26” and “Fiscal 2026”) is as follows:

    • Revenue: $392 million at the midpoint with a range of +/-2%, representing approximately 12% growth from previous year revenue vs. $373.45M consensus.
    • Adjusted EBITDA: Approximately $43 million at the midpoint of our revenue outlook.
    • Non-GAAP Diluted EPS: $0.16 at the midpoint of our revenue outlook vs. $0.14 consensus.

data.title: Target announces a collaboration with Kate Spade on a limited-time collection
data.description:
Target Corporation (NYSE:TGT) announced on Wednesday the launch of a limited-time collection of fashion, home and entertaining essentials with global lifestyle brand Kate Spade New York. The new collection was highlighted as bringing together the best of both brands by delivering aspirational designs and accessible style, while reinforcing the retailer's reputation for offering high-quality, stylish collaborations at an affordable value.

The new collection, which will launch on April 12, will feature more than 300 items, including women's, kids' and baby apparel (with extended sizing and adaptive styles), handbags, home accessories and entertaining must-haves. Over half of the collection is available for $15 and under, with prices starting at just $5. 

"With versatile pieces that work for every occasion and can't-miss prices, this partnership brings together kate spade's signature style with Target's legacy of making the best design accessible to all," said Jill Sando, Target's executive vice president and chief merchandising officer, apparel and accessories, home and hardlines.

The Kate Spade brand is owned by Tapestry (TPR).

Target's (NYSE:TGT) notable limited-time collections partners in the past have included arrangements with Kate Spade New York, Champion, Diane Von Furstenberg, Missoni, Lilly Pulitzer, Zac Posen, and Rowing Blazers.

Shares of Target (TGT) edged 0.2% higher in premarket trading on Wednesday. The retail stock is down 22.8% on a year-to-date basis.

data.title: Enovis names McDonald as CEO
data.description:
• Enovis (NYSE:ENOV) said it has appointed Damien McDonald as its CEO, effective May 12. 
  • McDonald will succeed Matt Trerotola, who previously announced his intention to retire as CEO.
  • The firm also reaffirmed its expectations for first-quarter revenues to be in the range of $555 million to $563 million and adjusted core profit in the range of $97 million to $100 million. 
  • McDonald had more than 35 years of experience in the medical device industry and most recently served as CEO of LivaNova.

data.title: Hyperscale Data unit mines 56 Bitcoin from Jan-March end
data.description:
• Hyperscale Data (NYSE:GPUS) said its unit, Sentinum, mined ~56 Bitcoin from January 1 to March 31.
  • The firm added that since March 2021, Sentinum has mined ~3,061 Bitcoin.
  • “The company has previously noted its intentions to relocate the majority of its Bitcoin mining operations concurrently with the buildout of its Michigan Data Center and will continue to update stockholders as this progresses,” said CEO William Horne.

data.title: AC Immune updates data from mid-stage trial for Parkinson’s candidate
data.description:
• AC Immune (NASDAQ:ACIU) added ~22% in the premarket on Wednesday after the Swiss biotech shared additional interim results from a mid-stage trial for ACI-7104.056, its experimental therapy targeted at the neurodegenerative disorder Parkinson’s disease.
  • Citing immunogenicity data from its Phase 2 VacSYn clinical trial, AC Immune (NASDAQ:ACIU) said ACI-7104.056 led to a high level of antibodies against alpha-synuclein, which is believed to play a major role in causing PD.
  • According to the company, after six months of treatment in the study for patients with early PD, the level of anti-a-syn antibodies seen following four immunizations of ACI-7104.056 was over 20-fold higher than that for placebo.
  • The experimental therapy was well tolerated without any safety issues so far, AC Immune (NASDAQ:ACIU) added, noting its plans to share additional updates later this year.

data.title: Analysts expect the Las Vegas Strip to have bounced back from a rough February
data.description: Las Vegas Strip casinos saw gaming win fall 13.8% year-over-year in February to $690 million due to a number of factors unrelated to consumer spending. The Las Vegas Strip faced a tough comparison to last year, when the Super Bowl brought significant traffic to Las Vegas a full week ahead of the game. There was also an extra day in February a year ago due to Leap Year and a gap in the convention rotation cycle that made last year's tally a little harder to match.Truist Securities analyst Barry Jonas noted that softer gaming revenue was also riven by a 52% decline in baccarat and 27% decline in table games. "That said, Strip casino win excluding baccarat was down just 3% year-over-year," he observed. "While we expected a softer February, underlying Vegas trends remain mostly positive for now and we expect to see improvements in March," he added.Macquarie analyst Chad Beynon also believes Vegas trends were stable in Q1. Beynon highlighted that group and convention bookings are rising, which when combined with several major capex projects ending could help Las Vegas property operators owners see EBITDA growth this year.Across Nevada, gaming win was down 9.3% in February to $1.28 billion. Gaming for table and card games plunged 22.0% to $401.7 million, while slot gaming win was down 1.4% to $816.0 million. Notably, baccarat gaming win down 50% during the month across Nevada and gaming win for sportsbooks was down 14.4% on a narrow win percentage of just 6.8%.Sector watch: Caesars Entertainment (NASDAQ:CZR), MGM Resorts (NYSE:MGM), Boyd Gaming (BYD), Wynn Resorts (WYNN), Full House Resorts (FLL), Bally's Corporation (BALY), and Red Rock Resorts (RRR).
